cases.publicSummary: Verified historical planning work for an automated strip-handling and blanking-feed concept, presented without employer, vendor, part, plant, pricing, production, or proprietary details.
cases.problem: Manual strip handling and press feeding required a repeatable sequence with clear setup, sensing, material-flow, exception, and review gates.
cases.role: Participated in project planning and evaluation, including proposal review, operating-sequence definition, integration questions, and facility/integrator evaluation.
decisions: use a staged feed sequence
require ready-state checks before automatic cycle
retain safety and tooling risks as engineering review items
cases.implementation: The historical record reached formal proposal, committee evaluation, concept drawings, and detailed order-of-operations documentation. Final commissioning is not claimed here.
cases.outcome: A corroborated planning and evaluation record exists. Exact labor, capacity, efficiency, safety, and financial outcomes remain held pending measured records.
cases.boundary: No proprietary sequence, source drawing, program, production value, vendor identity, plant identity, raw photo, or safety certification is published.
unresolvedQuestions: Locate independent commissioning and measured-result records before stating production outcomes.

cases.publicSummary: Verified historical planning work evaluating a robot-tended turning-center workflow, including part presentation, machine signals, probing, training, runoff, and implementation constraints.
cases.problem: An uneven machining workload created a need to evaluate unattended or multi-machine tending without losing control of part handling, quality, recovery, and operator responsibilities.
cases.role: Served as an owner-side planning contact and evaluator for the detailed system proposal and related probing, training, workflow, and implementation questions.
workPerformed: considered runoff, training, utilities, rigging, and part-family fit
decisions: treat part presentation and signal verification as primary gates
include probing and exception handling
separate planning evidence from final production claims
cases.implementation: The record includes a dated formal proposal, related project folder, machine-tending media, and probing quotation. Sole design ownership and final commissioning are not claimed.
cases.outcome: The planning role and technical evaluation are corroborated. Exact production rate, savings, implementation responsibility, and realized output remain held.
cases.boundary: No machine serial, part identity, program, screen, video, cycle data, vendor-specific control, price, or plant image is published.
unresolvedQuestions: Locate independent installation, acceptance, production, and financial records before stating final outcomes.

cases.publicSummary: A held future case study for bar utilization, remnant, cutoff, cycle, capacity, handling, and exception decisions.
cases.problem: Direct project evidence has not yet been separated from general machining and equipment records.
decisions: retain HOLD until a distinct evidence chain exists
cases.boundary: Do not publish production quantities, part numbers, programs, machine identity, or employer data.
unresolvedQuestions: Locate project-specific messages, setup records, programs, photos, proposals, or measured results.

cases.publicSummary: Historical records document responsibility for CNC programming support, FeatureCAM tool libraries and posts, programming consistency, troubleshooting, training, and fixture/process support.
cases.problem: Programming, tooling, posts, standards, troubleshooting, and knowledge transfer needed coordinated ownership to keep machining work repeatable.
cases.role: Supported and maintained programming workflows, tool libraries, posts, process consistency, junior programmers, machine troubleshooting, quality, fixtures, and tooling research.
decisions: use non-quantified public wording
keep programs, tooling inventories, parts, and internal operations private
cases.implementation: Responsibility records corroborate an established operating role rather than a one-time demonstration.
cases.outcome: The role and work scope are verified. Exact program count, output, scrap, savings, and customer results remain unapproved until independently counted or measured.
cases.boundary: No proprietary program, post, tool inventory, part number, employer identity, output value, or quantified career count is published.
unresolvedQuestions: A quantified program-count claim requires a reproducible inventory and methodology.

cases.internalTitle: CAD/CAM migration, macros and API work
cases.publicSummary: A public-safe historical planning summary covering CAD/CAM utilization, shared licensing, advanced macro training, reusable automation, file handling, and operator adoption without publishing proprietary files or exact savings.
cases.problem: Software capability was not being used consistently, while licensing, repetitive drawing and programming work, training, and errors created cost and throughput concerns.
cases.role: Evaluated workflow gaps and proposed training, shared access, macro development, process review, and more consistent CAD/CAM use.
decisions: present savings as historical estimates
hold exact software-version claims
keep macros, posts, licenses, files, and employer data private
cases.implementation: The accessible record supports planning and recommendation work, not a fully verified migration outcome.
cases.outcome: The modernization analysis is supported as a historical estimate. Exact version chronology, output doubling, labor savings, and avoided-error values remain held.
cases.boundary: Software names may describe personal experience; no proprietary file, license, macro, post, price, employer, or quantified result is published.
unresolvedQuestions: Locate dated exact-version records and independent implementation/results evidence before upgrading claims.

cases.publicSummary: Historical planning records show experience evaluating modernization across machining, automation, vision, CAD/CAM, monitoring, material flow, storage, and logistics.
cases.problem: Multiple production, handling, quality, information, storage, and staffing constraints needed to be prioritized as an integrated modernization portfolio.
cases.role: Researched, documented, compared, and helped sequence modernization opportunities while continuing machining and automation responsibilities.
workPerformed: connected automation, machining, vision, storage, and logistics
decisions: stage projects by readiness and learning value
separate proven work from research concepts
retain exact economics as private historical estimates
cases.implementation: The record shows repeated planning and evaluation across multiple initiatives; each final implementation remains subject to its own evidence chain.
cases.outcome: The breadth of modernization planning is corroborated. Named-company claims, exact project ownership, and quantified portfolio savings remain restricted or held.
cases.boundary: No former-employer name, customer, employee, drawing, price, program, production value, vendor identity, or plant detail is published.
unresolvedQuestions: Match each completed initiative to independent acceptance and measured-result records before publishing outcomes.

cases.publicSummary: A public-safe historical planning summary connecting vision inspection, bin-picking research, machine monitoring, material storage, staging, vertical storage, access, and workflow improvements.
cases.problem: Inspection, part orientation, machine utilization, material movement, staging, and storage created connected workflow constraints.
cases.role: Researched and documented vision, bin-picking, monitoring, storage, racking, and material-flow options as part of a broader modernization portfolio.
decisions: treat camera and storage options as staged evaluations
avoid publishing inspection criteria or plant layouts
retain projected savings as estimates
cases.implementation: Accessible evidence supports research and planning. Individual systems and final measured outcomes are not collectively claimed as complete.
cases.outcome: The cross-functional planning work is supported as a historical estimate; exact inspection performance, labor savings, storage gains, and installation responsibility remain held.
cases.boundary: No proprietary image, inspection criterion, plant layout, part, vendor, production value, safety certification, or raw photo is published.
unresolvedQuestions: Separate distinct completed projects and match each to independent acceptance and result records.

cases.publicSummary: Held pending owner-approved project records and photographs.
cases.problem: No usable project-specific evidence chain is available in the connected sources.
decisions: reject location-keyword matches that return private records
cases.outcome: No approved historical claim or asset.
cases.boundary: No location, address, owner, contractor, family, permit, utility, property record, or photo metadata may be published.
unresolvedQuestions: Owner must provide the original project folder and identify the photographs authorized for review.

cases.publicSummary: Held pending owner-approved project records and photographs.
cases.problem: Location searches returned private records rather than a project evidence chain.
decisions: exclude financial, medical, legal, utility, and property records from proof work
cases.outcome: No approved historical claim or asset.
cases.boundary: No address, family, customer, contractor, property record, financial record, or identifiable exterior view may be published.
unresolvedQuestions: Owner must provide the original project folder and identify the photographs authorized for review.

cases.publicSummary: A public-safe reconstruction informed by a historical planning packet containing a floor plan, elevations, material selections, and three-dimensional layout views.
cases.problem: Cabinet, appliance, circulation, work-zone, measurement, material, and sequencing decisions needed to be resolved before purchase or installation.
cases.role: Historical artifact confirms planning activity; exact authorship, customer relationship, and completed installation are not claimed.
decisions: remove vendor, store, design, item and price details
present only an anonymized planning reconstruction
avoid claiming permit or construction authority
cases.implementation: The public case study reconstructs the planning method rather than publishing the private source packet.
cases.outcome: The planning artifact is verified. Authorship, purchase, installation, property, customer, budget, and finished-result claims remain held.
cases.boundary: No vendor, store, design number, item number, price, address, customer, property, source rendering, or field-verified dimension is published.
unresolvedQuestions: Owner-approved original project photos are needed before any historical before/during/after sequence can be published.

cases.publicSummary: A 28-by-40-foot demonstration showing protected vehicle lanes, workbench and tool zones, fixed-obstacle checks, missing measurements, and phased setup actions.
cases.problem: A mixed-use garage needed clear vehicle, work, storage, utility, access, and future-project zones.
cases.role: Created a public demonstration from purpose-built planning inputs rather than customer evidence.
decisions: keep the example synthetic
separate planning from engineering and permit work
cases.outcome: A reusable planning example and customer-intake structure, not a verified construction outcome.
cases.boundary: Not customer work, a permit drawing, engineering, code verification, or field-verified construction plan.

cases.publicSummary: A seven-status owner-controlled workflow connecting intake, missing information, quote review, scheduling, completion, and follow-up without automatic customer sends.
cases.problem: Small-business work can become fragmented between inquiries, missing information, estimates, approvals, scheduling, delivery, and follow-up.
cases.role: Designed a synthetic public workflow example using no real customer or communication data.
decisions: use synthetic records only
keep customer actions owner-controlled
cases.implementation: Purpose-built demonstration inside the Highway 38 operating system.
cases.outcome: A reusable workflow model rather than a customer outcome claim.
cases.boundary: Synthetic records only; no real customer, message, address, financial, or private business data.
